title: The Complete Beginner's Guide to Padel in Marbella
excerpt: Everything you need to know to start playing padel in Marbella — from the rules and scoring to finding the right club and booking your first court.
author: María González
content:
What Is Padel and Why Is Everyone Playing It?

Padel is one of the world's fastest-growing racket sports, and nowhere is that growth more visible than in Marbella. A hybrid of tennis and squash played on an enclosed glass-and-mesh court roughly a third the size of a tennis court, padel is deceptively easy to pick up but endlessly rewarding to master. The sport is played in doubles, and the use of the back walls dramatically extends rallies — making it much more accessible for beginners than traditional tennis.

Marbella's year-round sunshine and thriving international community have turned the Costa del Sol into one of Europe's padel capitals. With over 50 clubs within a short drive of the town centre, you'll have no trouble finding a court at any time of day.

Understanding the Rules and Scoring

Padel uses the same scoring system as tennis — 15, 30, 40, game — and matches are typically played as best of three sets. The key differences are the walls: after the ball bounces once on your side, it can rebound off the glass or wire mesh and still be played legally. This keeps rallies alive and rewards creative shot-making over raw power.

  • Serve underhand or at waist height, diagonally into the service box
  • After the bounce, use the glass walls to extend rallies
  • The ball must bounce on the ground before hitting the back wall after a service return
  • Volleys are allowed anywhere in the court — a key difference from squash

What Equipment Do You Need?

To play padel you need a padel racket (shorter and solid compared to a tennis racket), padel balls (slightly less pressurised than tennis balls), and padel shoes with herringbone-patterned soles for the artificial grass surface. Most clubs in Marbella offer racket and ball hire for around €3–5, so you don't need to invest upfront for your first few sessions.

Booking Your First Court in Marbella

The easiest way to book a court in Marbella is through the Playtomic app, which most clubs use for their online reservation system. Simply download the app, search for clubs near you, and choose a free slot. Many clubs also accept walk-in bookings, especially during weekday off-peak hours. Expect to pay between €10 and €40 per court per hour depending on the club, time of day, and whether the court is indoor or outdoor.

Tips for Your First Game

Don't be intimidated — padel is genuinely one of the most beginner-friendly racket sports. Focus on consistency rather than power, stay close to the net in the attack position, and communicate with your partner. Most importantly, enjoy the social aspect: padel culture in Marbella is famously welcoming, and you'll likely find yourself sharing a drink with your opponents at the club bar after the match.

title: The 9 Best Padel Clubs in Marbella for 2026
excerpt: We visited every major padel club in Marbella to bring you the definitive guide — covering court quality, pricing, facilities, and the overall experience.
author: Carlos Ramos
content:
How We Ranked Marbella's Padel Clubs

Over the past three months we visited every significant padel club in the greater Marbella area — from Estepona in the west to Los Monteros in the east. We evaluated each venue on five criteria: court quality and surface condition, facilities and amenities, value for money, location and accessibility, and the overall social atmosphere. The result is the most comprehensive padel club guide on the Costa del Sol.

The Standout Venues

At the very top of our list sits NAC Nueva Alcántara in San Pedro Alcántara. With 16 courts, a professional coaching academy, and a social calendar packed with tournaments, NAC offers the most complete padel experience in Marbella. Puente Romano Padel is our pick for the most exclusive experience — eight impeccably maintained outdoor courts within the five-star resort's grounds, complete with post-game spa access.

  • NAC Nueva Alcántara — Best overall, best for serious players
  • Puente Romano Padel — Best luxury experience
  • Real Club Padel Marbella — Best for community and social play
  • Manolo Santana Racquets Club — Best in Nueva Andalucía
  • Estepona Padel Center — Best indoor facility

Best Value Options

For budget-conscious players, San Pedro Padel Club and Los Monteros Racket Club offer excellent court quality at very competitive prices. San Pedro in particular has a loyal local following and regularly runs beginner clinics that represent exceptional value.

Booking Tips for Peak Season

During July and August, Marbella's padel courts are in extremely high demand. We recommend booking through Playtomic at least 48–72 hours in advance for popular clubs like NAC and Puente Romano. Weekday morning slots (before 10:00) and early evening slots (after 20:00) tend to have more availability year-round. Several clubs also offer monthly membership packages that include guaranteed court time — well worth considering if you plan to stay for more than a couple of weeks.

The Verdict

Marbella's padel scene is genuinely world-class. Whether you're a first-timer looking for a casual hit or a seasoned player seeking competitive league play, the Costa del Sol has a club that perfectly suits your needs. We'll be updating this guide throughout 2026 as new facilities open and existing clubs improve their offerings.

title: World Padel Tour Returns to Marbella in 2026
excerpt: The prestigious World Padel Tour is back on the Costa del Sol this spring. Here's everything you need to know about attending the Marbella Master.
author: Sofía Martín
content:
The Marbella Master: One of Padel's Most Prestigious Stops

The Marbella Master is one of the most eagerly anticipated events on the professional padel calendar. Drawing the world's top players to the Costa del Sol each spring, the tournament combines world-class padel action with the glamour and sunshine that Marbella does so well. The 2026 edition promises to be the biggest yet, with upgraded facilities and an expanded spectator programme.

When and Where

The 2026 Marbella Master takes place from April 15 to 19 at the Puente Romano Beach Resort. The iconic glass courts will be set up on the resort's beachside terrace, creating a spectacular backdrop for the action. Gates open at 10:00 each day, with the main draw matches typically beginning at 11:00.

  • Dates: 15–19 April 2026
  • Venue: Puente Romano Beach Resort, Marbella
  • Tickets: From €25 per day (ground pass) to €180 (VIP package)
  • Stars to watch: Juan Lebrón, Alejandro Galán, Gemma Triay, Alejandra Salazar

Getting Tickets

Tickets for the 2026 Marbella Master go on sale on March 25 via the official World Padel Tour website. Day passes offer access to all courts and the main arena, while the VIP package includes a dedicated lounge, complimentary food and drinks, and the chance to attend the players' gala dinner. We strongly recommend booking early — the event sold out in 2025 and demand is even higher this year.

Around the Tournament: Padel Activities for All

Beyond the professional matches, the tournament village will feature a padel demo court where fans can try the sport under the supervision of qualified coaches, a large pro shop with the latest equipment, and a fan zone with food, music, and padel-themed activities for children. It's a fantastic day out even if you've never picked up a padel racket before.

title: How to Choose the Right Padel Racket in 2026
excerpt: With hundreds of padel rackets on the market, choosing the right one can feel overwhelming. Our expert guide breaks down everything you need to know.
author: David López
content:
Understanding Padel Racket Shapes

The most important decision when buying a padel racket is choosing the right shape. There are three main shapes: round, teardrop, and diamond. Round rackets have a larger sweet spot and are more forgiving — ideal for beginners and intermediate players. Diamond-shaped rackets are harder to control but generate enormous power — favoured by advanced players with precise technique. Teardrop shapes offer a balance between control and power, making them the most popular choice for club players of all levels.

Materials and Construction

Modern padel rackets are made from combinations of fibreglass, carbon fibre, and EVA or FOAM rubber cores. Fibreglass faces are more flexible and forgiving, while carbon fibre offers precision and responsiveness. The core material significantly affects the feel: soft EVA foam provides a more comfortable, arm-friendly experience, while harder foam generates more direct power feedback. High-end rackets from brands like Bullpadel, Head, and Babolat use premium carbon layups and memory rubber cores for the optimal combination of performance and feel.

  • Beginners: Round shape, fibreglass, soft foam core — €50–120
  • Intermediate: Teardrop shape, carbon/fibreglass hybrid — €100–200
  • Advanced: Diamond or teardrop, full carbon — €180–400+

Top Racket Recommendations for 2026

For beginners, the Bullpadel Vertex 04 Control is an outstanding choice — comfortable, durable, and available in most Marbella pro shops for around €80. Intermediate players will love the Head Zephyr, which offers exceptional touch and a large sweet spot at a very competitive price. Advanced players should consider the Babolat Technical Viper, a precision instrument used by several World Padel Tour professionals.

Where to Buy in Marbella

The best selection of padel rackets in Marbella can be found at the pro shops of NAC Nueva Alcántara, Manolo Santana Racquets Club, and Puente Romano. Most clubs also offer demo programmes where you can test a racket for one session before committing to a purchase — a service we strongly recommend taking advantage of before spending significant money.

Caring for Your Racket

Padel rackets are more delicate than they look. Avoid hitting the ground or glass walls with the frame, store your racket in a protective case, and keep it away from extreme heat (such as a car boot in summer). A good protective tape around the frame's edge is a worthwhile investment that can extend your racket's life considerably.

title: NAC Nueva Alcántara: An In-Depth Review of Marbella's Best Padel Club
excerpt: We spent a full week at NAC Nueva Alcántara to bring you the most thorough review of Marbella's largest and most acclaimed padel complex.
author: María González
content:
First Impressions

Arriving at NAC Nueva Alcántara for the first time, you immediately understand why this club has become the benchmark for padel in southern Spain. The complex sprawls across several acres in the San Pedro Alcántara urbanisation, with 16 courts — a mix of indoor and outdoor — visible from the entrance. The facility is immaculately maintained, and the energy is palpable from the moment you walk through the doors.

The Courts

NAC's courts are divided between a climate-controlled indoor hall housing six courts and ten outdoor courts surrounded by tall fencing and floodlights for evening play. All surfaces are premium artificial grass in excellent condition, replaced on a strict rotation schedule to ensure consistent bounce and traction. The outdoor courts benefit from the club's sheltered location, which reduces wind interference even on breezy coastal days.

  • 6 indoor courts (climate controlled, LED lighting)
  • 10 outdoor courts (floodlit until 23:00)
  • All courts: premium artificial grass, Agility brand
  • Glass walls: 10mm tempered, exceptional rebound consistency

Facilities and Amenities

The clubhouse is a modern, spacious building housing changing rooms with high-quality showers, a fully-stocked pro shop, a physiotherapy clinic, and a restaurant-bar that serves proper food until late. The bar area is particularly lively after matches, with a genuine community feel that you don't always find at larger clubs. The pro shop stocks all the major padel brands with knowledgeable staff who can help you find the right equipment.

Coaching and Programmes

NAC's coaching academy is one of the finest on the Costa del Sol. Led by former WPT professional Javier Morales, the team of six coaches offers group clinics for all levels, private lessons, and a junior academy that has produced several nationally ranked players. The weekly social ladder tournament on Wednesday evenings is particularly well organised and a great way to meet other players at your level.

Value and Booking

Court hire runs from €18 to €28 per hour depending on the time slot and whether you choose indoor or outdoor. By Marbella standards this is extremely competitive given the quality on offer. Booking through Playtomic is smooth and reliable, and the club's staff are responsive via WhatsApp for any queries. NAC deserves its reputation as the best all-round padel club in Marbella.

title: 10 Proven Tips to Rapidly Improve Your Padel Game
excerpt: Whether you've been playing for six months or six years, these expert-backed tips will help you take your padel game to the next level.
author: Carlos Ramos
content:
Master the Basics Before Chasing Tricks

The most common mistake intermediate padel players make is neglecting the fundamentals in favour of flashy shots. A reliable, consistent forehand and backhand volley, a solid defensive lob, and a well-placed serve will win you far more matches than a spectacular but unreliable bandeja. Book a session with a qualified coach and have them video your technique — you'll likely be surprised by what you see.

Positioning Is Everything

In padel, where you stand on the court matters as much as how you hit the ball. The golden rule: attack from the net, defend from the back. When your team is in the attacking position (both players at the net), stay tight to avoid giving your opponents angles. When defending, maintain a deep position with at least one player ready to use the back wall.

  • Attack: Both players at the net, T-formation
  • Defence: Both players deep, one covering each side
  • Transition: Move together — never leave a gap down the middle
  • Communication: Call every ball that crosses the centre line

Learn to Love the Walls

The walls are your friend, not your enemy. Rather than trying to win every point with direct winners, practise using the back glass to reset defensive positions and create unexpected angles. The chiquita (a soft, low shot into the corners) and the vibora (a sharp spinning volley off the glass) are two advanced shots that become devastating weapons once mastered.

Improve Your Physical Conditioning

Padel requires a unique combination of explosive lateral movement, rotational power, and cardiovascular endurance. Lateral agility drills, rotational medicine ball exercises, and interval training will all translate directly to better on-court performance. Many of Marbella's clubs offer padel-specific fitness programmes — NAC Nueva Alcántara's physical conditioning course is particularly well regarded.

Play with Better Players

The fastest way to improve at any sport is to play regularly with people who are better than you. Don't be intimidated — the padel community in Marbella is extremely welcoming, and most advanced players are happy to include a lower-level partner in a social match. Seek out the social ladder evenings at your club, join a beginner-friendly league, and sign up for open tournaments. The combination of match pressure and exposure to superior players will accelerate your progress dramatically.
